topic.name: Contemporary Music Analysis
topic.description: This topic encompasses the exploration and critique of various contemporary songs and albums across different genres, focusing on their themes, reception, and cultural impact.

Document 3: The lyrics explore mortality and aging.
The song received highly positive reviews upon its release.

topic.description: A discussion of various video game genres, their development history, and gameplay mechanics.

set in a post-apocalyptic version of the United States

setting of the main series is a Northern European-style dark fantasy world

players use a guitar-shaped game controller to simulate playing lead, bass guitar, and rhythm guitar

topic.name: Crime and Political Movements
topic.description: The documents discuss organized crime activities and political movements in different regions, focusing on their historical context and implications.

Document 1: The Abergil crime family is a Moroccan-Jewish crime organization based in Israel, involved in drug trafficking, murder, extortion, and other crimes.
Considered one of the six major organized-crime cells in Israel, and one of the world’s top 40 biggest drug importers to the United States.
Document 2: The Democratic Left Movement is a nonsectarian and a democratic leftist political party with seats in the Lebanese Parliament.
It participated in the 2005 Cedar Revolution, a wave of demonstrations against the Syrian occupation of Lebanon.

topic.name: Cultural Expressions and Identity
topic.description: The documents explore various forms of cultural expression, ranging from language and literature to sociological studies of communities and identities.

Document 1: Pe ̍ h-ōe-jī is an orthography used to write variants of Southern Min Chinese
A large amount of printed material has been produced in the script
Document 2: It 's a Good Life, If You Don 't Weaken is a graphic novel by Canadian cartoonist Seth
The story unfolds with a nostalgic and melancholic tone
Document 3: A Community of Witches is a sociological study of the Wiccan and wider Pagan community
Berger undertook fieldwork in both a local Wiccan coven and a wider Pagan organisation

topic.name: Atlantic and Pacific Hurricanes
topic.description: The documents discuss various hurricanes and tropical storms in the Atlantic and Pacific regions, detailing their formation, intensity, impacts, and historical significance.

Document 1: Hurricane Arlene was the first tropical cyclone of the 1963 Atlantic hurricane season
the storm has its greatest impact in Bermuda where high winds and near-record rainfall downed trees, power lines and caused flooding.
Document 2: The 1949 Atlantic hurricane season was the last season that tropical cyclones were not publicly named
Overall, the season featured 16 tropical storms and seven hurricanes.
Document 3: Hurricane Sergio was the third strongest Pacific hurricane in the month of November on record
Sergio produced light rainfall along the coast of Mexico, though its effects were minimal.

topic.name: Formula One and Racing History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history, regulations, and notable events of Formula One racing and other racing series, highlighting key races, drivers, and vehicles.

Document 1: Lancia LC2 was part of Lancia's official factory-backed effort in the World Sportscar Championship
They were also the company 's first car meeting the FIA 's new Group C regulations for sports prototypes.
Document 2: The 2015 Monaco Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 24 May 2015
Nico Rosberg took his third consecutive win at Monaco.
Document 3: The 2015 Italian Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 6 September 2015
Hamilton completed the second Grand Slam of his career by winning the race.

topic.name: Film and Cinema History
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of film production, notable actors, and historical films spanning different eras.

Document 1: Saving Mr. Banks is a 2013 period drama film directed by John Lee Hancock
the film stars Emma Thompson as author P. L. Travers and Tom Hanks as filmmaker Walt Disney
Document 2: Leonardo Wilhelm DiCaprio is an American actor and a film producer
DiCaprio gained public recognition with leading roles in The Basketball Diaries and the romantic drama Romeo + Juliet
Document 3: The Testament of Dr. Mabuse is a 1933 German crime film directed by Fritz Lang
the film features Rudolf Klein-Rogge as Dr. Mabuse who is in an insane asylum

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the annual rowing competition between the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge, detailing specific races and their outcomes.

Document 1: The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge.
Oxford beat Cambridge by a distance of four-and-a-half lengths.
Document 2: The Boat Race is an annual side-by-side rowing race along the River Thames between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge.
Oxford won by two-and-a-quarter lengths in a time of 20 minutes 50 seconds.
Document 3: The Boat Race is an annual rowing eight competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.
Oxford won by a margin of three-and-three-quarter lengths in a time of 21 minutes 23 seconds.

topic.name: Historical Commemoration and Records
topic.description: This topic encompasses the commemoration of historical figures and events, as well as the preservation of financial and governance records from different historical periods.

1: Bosa was an Anglo-Saxon Bishop of York during the 7th and early 8th centuries.
He subsequently appears as a saint in an 8th-century liturgical calendar.
2: The Confederate Memorial commemorates residents of Hampshire County who died during the American Civil War.
The town of Romney has claimed that this is the first memorial structure erected to memorialize the Confederate dead in the United States.
3: The Pipe rolls are a collection of financial records maintained by the English Exchequer.
They form the oldest continuous series of records concerning English governance kept by the English, British and United Kingdom governments.

topic.name: Historical Military Events
topic.description: The documents discuss significant military events and figures from ancient and medieval history.

Document 1: Marianos Argyros was a Byzantine aristocrat and member of the Argyros family.
He held a succession of senior military commands, fighting in southern Italy against local rebels and the Fatimids.
Document 2: The Siege of Naxos was a failed attempt by the Milesian tyrant Aristagoras to conquer the island of Naxos.
It was the opening act of the Greco-Persian Wars, which would ultimately last for 50 years.

topic.name: Democratic Political Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss notable figures and events within the Democratic Party in the United States, focusing on their political careers and electoral campaigns.

Document 1: Alton B. Parker was an American judge, best known as the Conservative Democrat who lost the presidential election of 1904.
Parker practiced law in Kingston, New York, before being appointed to the New York Supreme Court.
Document 2: Mary Jo Kilroy is the former U.S. Representative for Ohio's 15th congressional district, serving from 2009 until 2011.
She is a member of the Democratic Party from Ohio.
Document 3: The Joe Biden presidential campaign, 2008 began when Biden, then the senior Senator from Delaware, announced his candidacy for President of the United States.
Biden had previously run for president in 1988, but failed to receive the nomination of the Democratic Party.

topic.name: State Highways in North America
topic.description: This cluster discusses various state highways in North America, including their routes, historical development, and geographical significance.

Document 1: M-68 is an east – west state trunkline highway located in the northern part of the Lower Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
The current designation was created in 1936.
Document 2: Maryland Route 10 ( MD 10 ) is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
MD 10 was constructed from I-695 to MD 710 in the early 1970s.
Document 3: King 's Highway 127 , commonly referred to as Highway 127 , is a provincially maintained highway in the Canadian province of Ontario.
Highway 127 was designated in late 1955, generally following the same route as today.

topic.name: Benthic Marine Species
topic.description: This topic encompasses various species of benthic marine animals, including electric rays and stingrays, highlighting their characteristics, habitats, and biological features.

Document 1: Norfolk Spaniel is an extinct breed of dog
described as being a large cocker spaniel.
Document 2: onefin electric ray is a common but little-known species
found in shallow coastal bays over sandy or muddy bottoms.
Document 3: sparsely-spotted stingaree is a species of stingray
preferring sandy flats and seagrass beds.

topic.name: Historical Baseball and Cricket Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss notable players from the sports of cricket and baseball, highlighting their careers and significant achievements.

Document 1: M. P. Bajana was an Indian amateur cricketer who played 55 first-class cricket matches between 1911 and 1920.
Bajana scored three centuries, and made his highest score in 1920, scoring 115 runs against Cambridge University.
Document 2: Ned Williamson was an American Major League Baseball player for 13 seasons from 1878 until 1890.
Williamson held the single-season record for both doubles and home runs from 1883 to 1887.

topic.name: Watershed and Stream Ecology
topic.description: The documents discuss various streams and their ecological significance, including their hydrological features, human impact, and surrounding environments.

Document 1: Mahanoy Creek is a 51.6-mile-long tributary of the Susquehanna River
There are at least 35 sources of acid mine drainage in the creek's watershed.
Document 2: Neepaulakating Creek is a 2.4-mile long tributary of Papakating Creek
the stream flows into Lake Neepaulin located at 509 ft above sea level.

topic.name: Military Medical and Training Units
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history and roles of military medical facilities and training divisions within the armed forces, particularly during major conflicts such as World War I and World War II.

Document 1: The 100th Division is an infantry division of the United States Army headquartered at Fort Knox, Kentucky.
It primarily took on numerous training roles for other Army units.
Document 2: The Australian Voluntary Hospital was a military hospital staffed by Australian expatriates in England that served on the Western Front between 1914 and 1916.
It was the only Australian presence on the Western Front.
Document 3: The 3rd Brigade, 7th Infantry Division was activated for service in World War I.
It saw brief service in the conflict, but never fought as an entire unit.

topic.name: Music Albums and Compositions
topic.description: The documents discuss various music albums and compositions from different genres and time periods.

Document 1: Six moments musicaux is a set of solo piano pieces composed by Sergei Rachmaninoff.
Each piece stands on its own as a concert solo with individual themes and moods.
Document 2: Satisfied is the fourth studio album by American Christian rock band DecembeRadio.
The album debuted at its peak positions of number 116 on the Billboard 200 and number three on the Billboard Top Christian Albums charts.
Document 3: At Newport 1960 is a live album by Muddy Waters performed at Newport Jazz Festival.
The album is said to be one of the first live blues albums.

topic.name: Historic Sports Events
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant achievements and milestones in various sports, highlighting record-setting performances and notable competitions.

final contest of the 2002 NCAA Division I-A football season

topic.name: World War II Aviation History
topic.description: The documents discuss significant aircraft and pilots from World War II, highlighting their contributions and developments in military aviation.

Document 1: James Archibald Findlay MacLachlan DSO, DFC & Two Bars was a Royal Air Force (RAF) fighter pilot and flying ace of the Second World War.
MacLachlan was credited with 16 German and Italian aircraft shot down in approximately 250 missions.
Document 2: The Ryan FR Fireball was a mixed-power fighter aircraft designed by Ryan Aeronautical for the United States Navy during World War II.
It was the Navy's first aircraft with a jet engine.
Document 3: The Lockheed AH-56 Cheyenne was an attack helicopter developed by Lockheed for the United States Army.
It rose from the Army's Advanced Aerial Fire Support System (AAFSS) program to field the service's first dedicated attack helicopter.

topic.name: Occupational Health and Safety
topic.description: The documents focus on various aspects of occupational health and safety, including monitoring pesticide exposure, electrical impedance in safety equipment, and decompression practices for divers.

Document 1: SENSOR-Pesticides is a U.S. state-based surveillance program that monitors pesticide-related illness and injury.
Pesticide-related illness is a significant occupational health issue, but it is believed to be underreported.
Document 2: Nominal impedance in electrical engineering and audio engineering refers to the approximate designed impedance of an electrical circuit or device.
Document 3: The practice of decompression by divers comprises the planning and monitoring of the profile indicated by the algorithms or tables of the chosen decompression model.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various forms of transportation infrastructure, including roller coasters and railway stations.

Document 1: Maverick is a steel roller coaster built by Intamin at Cedar Point
Maverick was scheduled to open on May 12, 2007
Document 2: Sea Mills railway station is on the Severn Beach Line
The station was opened in 1865 by the Bristol Port Railway and Pier
Document 3: The Lier Line is an abandoned railway line that ran through Lier in Norway
The line opened on 12 July 1904 and was initially profitable

topic.name: American Television Episodes
topic.description: The documents discuss individual episodes from various American television series, highlighting their plots, characters, and production details.

Document 1: 'Entrada' is the eighth episode of the third season of the American science fiction drama television series Fringe
'Entrada' was the first episode of the season to have time evenly divided between both.
Document 2: 'Hi, Infidelity' is the sixth episode of the third season of the American mystery television series Veronica Mars
The series depicts the adventures of Veronica Mars as she deals with life as a college student while moonlighting as a private detective.
Document 3: 'Terra Prime' is the 21st episode of the fourth season of the American science fiction television series Star Trek: Enterprise
Set in the 22nd century, the series follows the adventures of the first Starfleet starship Enterprise.

topic.name: Naval Vessels of World War I
topic.description: The documents discuss various naval vessels, their construction, service history, and roles during World War I and earlier conflicts.

Document 1: Brummer class consisted of two light mine-laying cruisers built for the Imperial German Navy in World War I
Both vessels were interned at Scapa Flow after the end of the war
Document 2: Italia was an Italian ironclad battleship build for the Italian Regia Marina
During the Italo-Turkish War of 1911 – 12, the ship provided fire support to Italian troops defending Tripoli in Libya
Document 3: HMS Neptune was an ironclad turret ship originally designed and built in Britain for Brazil
Neptune unintentionally rammed HMS Victory, then serving as a training hulk for the Naval Signal School

topic.name: Olympic Participation of Small Nations
topic.description: This topic covers the participation of small nations in the Olympic Games, focusing on their athletes, events, and historical context.

Document 2: The Solomon Islands send a team to compete at the 2008 Summer Olympics in Beijing, China.
The country 's delegation consisted of three athletes competing in two sports across three distinct events.
Document 3: Nauru competed at the 2008 Summer Olympics, that were celebrated in Beijing, China.
Nauru was represented by the Nauru Olympic Committee, and was the only nation out of 204 participating nations and territories that sent only a single athlete.

topic.name: Notable Figures in Literature and Arts
topic.description: This topic encompasses influential individuals in the fields of literature and performing arts, highlighting their contributions and life stories.

Document 1: Shangguan Yunzhu was a Chinese actress active from the 1940s to the 1960s.
She starred in several prominent leftist films such as Spring River Flows East, Crows and Sparrows, and Women Side by Side.
Document 2: John Edmund Gardner was an English spy and thriller novelist, best known for his James Bond continuation novels.
Gardner wrote over fifty works of fiction, including fourteen original James Bond novels.
